title: Putin faces a series of setbacks, fueling discontent in Russia and raising doubts about his strategy
subhead: While the Russian president claims that victory in Ukraine is within reach, Kyiv is stepping up drone strikes deep inside Russian territory. These military setbacks compound the country's mounting economic difficulties.

title: Trump says Israel and Hezbollah agree to halt fighting
Why consumer electronics prices are surging
French presidential election: 'Which of Attal or Glucksmann can form the broadest coalition'
subhead: Does the social-democratic space still have a future, or is it destined to merge into a broad central bloc? That is one of the key issues in the presidential pre-campaign that has just begun, writes Le Monde columnist Françoise Fressoz.

title: Sweden's right-wing and far-right coalition backs down on deportations of young adults
subhead: The media coverage of several cases involving young people who grew up in Sweden and were forced to leave their families has shocked public opinion. Facing pressure in the polls, the government is revising the rules.

title: French government restricts Israel's official participation in the Eurosatory defense trade show
subhead: Only companies exhibiting missile defense or anti-aircraft systems are allowed. The Israeli defense ministry is protesting what it calls a 'disgraceful' decision, which was already enforced at the Paris Air Show in June 2025.

title: The Fontainebleau Forest's ever-growing popularity threatens this jewel of biodiversity near Paris
subhead: Managers of the forest complex are wondering how to deal with the increasing influx of visitors: Will development contain overtourism or encourage it?

Elon Musk, SpaceX and the illusion of wealth
subhead: The upcoming stock market listing of SpaceX values the company, of which the billionaire owns less than 50%, at nearly $2 trillion. This figure relies on the success of projects that are unrealistic within any reasonable financial timeframe, such as establishing a colony on Mars or building data centers in space.

title: French sociologist, philosopher and intellectual provocateur Edgar Morin has died aged 104
subhead: Committed to connecting different fields of knowledge to develop his 'complex thought,' this former member of the Resistance and deeply humanist intellectual advocated for a revolution of consciousness. He died in Paris on Friday, his wife confirmed to Le Monde.

title: The Chinese women striving to escape societal pressures to marry and have children
subhead: From climbing gyms to specialty bookstores and entrepreneur clubs, several women-focused spaces have opened in China's major cities. These places of solidarity offer women ways to push back against pressures to have children in a country where feminist activism is closely monitored.
